Caption: Optical images of (a) NIPAM-52 and (b) egg-white-based hydrogel phantoms.
Schematic of the HIFU ablation system.

Evolution of optical transparency and lesion formation of NIPAM-52 hydrogel phantom at different time points during a HIFU heating–cooling cycle (HIFU operating at 23.9W acoustic power; turning on and off at t =61 and 120s, respectively).

Evolution of optical transparency and lesion formation of egg-white-based hydrogel phantom at different time points during a HIFU heating–cooling cycle (HIFU operating at 23.9W acoustic power; turning on and off at t =61 and 120s, respectively).
The (a) area and (b) length of thermal lesions formed in various hydrogel phantoms and swine tissues under HIFU ablation at different acoustic powers for 120s.
Optical images of thermal lesions formed in (a) NIPAM-52 hydrogel phantom, (b) pork tenderloin, (c) swine brain and (d) egg-white-based hydrogel phantom under identical HIFU conditions.

Evolution of the (a) area and (b) length of thermal lesion formed in NIPAM-based hydrogel phantom with increasing AAc volume at an HIFU acoustic power of 30.4W for 120s. Shaded areas in (a) and (b): the area and length of thermal lesions (±1σ) formed in pork tenderloin and swine brain under identical HIFU conditions.

Levels of material damage induced in NIPAM-52 hydrogel phantom under different combinations of HIFU acoustic power and ablation duration. The upper and lower boundary lines indicate the approximate thresholds of inducing permanent and transient bubbles, respectively.

Images of four levels of material damage induced by HIFU in NIPAM-52 hydrogel phantom: (a) No–Bubbles, (b) Bubbles–During–HIFU, (c) Bubbles–After–HIFU and (d) Lasting–Bubbles. (a) and (b) are B-mode ultrasound images; (c) and (d) are optical images.
